content.text: CI dry-builds three NixOS hosts and four home configurations. I'll eval the classic-laddie toplevel plus all four home configs.

input.command: nix eval --raw .#nixosConfigurations.classic-laddie.config.system.build.toplevel.drvPath 2>&1 | tail -1; echo; for h in "personal" "paflynn@bushmills" "patrick@crostini" "paflynn@crostini"; do printf '%s: ' "$h"; nix eval --raw ".#homeConfigurations.\"$h\".activationPackage.drvPath" 2>&1 | tail -1; echo; done

input.command:
gh pr create --reviewer patflynn --title "home/dev: install antigravity CLI alongside the IDE" --body "Extends the \`cosmo.antigravity.enable\` gate in \`home/dev.nix\` to also install \`pkgs.antigravity-cli\` (antigravity-cli-1.0.12 in the pinned nixpkgs), so enabling Antigravity brings in both the IDE and its CLI.

Run: 20260715-0856-44a57cb4"

content.content: https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/635

content.text:
Done — PR #635: https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/635

**What changed:** `home/dev.nix` now installs `pkgs.antigravity-cli` alongside `pkgs.antigravity`, both behind the existing `cosmo.antigravity.enable` gate (converted `lib.optional … pkgs.antigravity` to `lib.optionals … [ pkgs.antigravity pkgs.antigravity-cli ]`, and updated the option description to mention the CLI).

**Verification:**
- Confirmed the attribute exists in the pinned nixpkgs first: `pkgs.antigravity-cli` evaluates to `antigravity-cli-1.0.12` (no guessing needed).
- `nix flake check` passed, including the pre-commit check.
- Evaluated the `classic-laddie` toplevel and all four CI-built home configurations (`personal`, `paflynn@bushmills`, `patrick@crostini`, `paflynn@crostini`) — all produce derivations.
- Confirmed classic-laddie (the host that enables the flag) now gets both `antigravity-1.23.2` and `antigravity-cli-1.0.12` in its home packages.

**The predicted conflict happened:** the concurrent delta/difftastic work merged to main as #634 while I was working, touching the same file. I rebased onto origin/main before pushing — the rebase was clean (different sections of the file) — and re-ran the evals on the rebased tree. `klaus _pre-review` reported no issues.

result:
Done — PR #635: https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/635

**What changed:** `home/dev.nix` now installs `pkgs.antigravity-cli` alongside `pkgs.antigravity`, both behind the existing `cosmo.antigravity.enable` gate (converted `lib.optional … pkgs.antigravity` to `lib.optionals … [ pkgs.antigravity pkgs.antigravity-cli ]`, and updated the option description to mention the CLI).

**Verification:**
- Confirmed the attribute exists in the pinned nixpkgs first: `pkgs.antigravity-cli` evaluates to `antigravity-cli-1.0.12` (no guessing needed).
- `nix flake check` passed, including the pre-commit check.
- Evaluated the `classic-laddie` toplevel and all four CI-built home configurations (`personal`, `paflynn@bushmills`, `patrick@crostini`, `paflynn@crostini`) — all produce derivations.
- Confirmed classic-laddie (the host that enables the flag) now gets both `antigravity-1.23.2` and `antigravity-cli-1.0.12` in its home packages.

**The predicted conflict happened:** the concurrent delta/difftastic work merged to main as #634 while I was working, touching the same file. I rebased onto origin/main before pushing — the rebase was clean (different sections of the file) — and re-ran the evals on the rebased tree. `klaus _pre-review` reported no issues.
